novel_features.name: Today's slices across all stores
novel_features.description: See which Pagliacci slices are available right now at every Seattle store, sorted by proximity to your saved address.
novel_features.rationale: Requires syncing MenuSlices for all 8+ stores into the local store and joining by location. Pagliacci's web UI shows slices one store at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent is asked 'what slices can I get tonight?', this returns a single comparable list — no per-store iteration needed.

novel_features.name: Open stores tonight
novel_features.description: List stores that are still open and can deliver to your saved address right now, sorted by ETA.
novel_features.rationale: Requires real-time TimeWindowDays + Store.OpenHour + delivery-zone resolution. Pagliacci's UI lists all stores regardless of cutoff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Late-night ordering: the agent only surfaces stores that will actually take the order.

novel_features.name: Stack discounts to maximize savings
novel_features.description: Compute the best application of stored coupons, reward redemption, and account credit for a given order total. Defaults to single-best-coupon + credit; multi-coupon stacking is flagged --experimental.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining StoredCoupons + RewardCard + StoredCredit and applying optimal-application logic. The site applies coupons one at a time at checkout.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents helping with order placement can pick the optimal discount, not just the first valid coupon.

novel_features.name: Reorder last (or by ID)
novel_features.description: Re-create a past order as a fresh cart, with price revalidation since prices change. Add --send to also submit.
novel_features.rationale: Composes OrderListItem + OrderClone + OrderPrice. The site has a one-click reorder but exposes no batch mode.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can replay a routine order without rebuilding the cart line by line.

novel_features.name: Address-aware delivery time picker
novel_features.description: Resolve a saved address label to the next available delivery slot in one call.
novel_features.rationale: Joins AddressName + AddressInfo zone resolution + TimeWindows lookup.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents scheduling orders for a specific address don't need to discover the delivery zone separately.

novel_features.name: Spend summary
novel_features.description: Aggregate order spend over a time range, with top items and store breakdown.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates OrderListItem locally. The site lets you browse history one order at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents helping with budgeting or reimbursement can produce a single roll-up report.

narrative.headline: Order Seattle's favorite pizza from the terminal — every endpoint, plus discount stacking, slice rotation across stores, and a local order history nobody else has.
narrative.value_prop: First and only CLI for the Pagliacci API. Browse menus and slice availability across all Seattle stores, build and price orders, manage your reward card and stored coupons, and replay past orders — all with offline search, agent-native output, and Chrome-cookie login (no manual token paste).
narrative.auth_narrative: Pagliacci has no public API and uses a custom composed `PagliacciAuth {customerId}|{authToken}` header constructed from cookies. Run `pagliacci-pp-cli auth login --chrome` while logged into pagliacci.com in Chrome — the CLI reads the auth cookies and constructs the header for you. No manual token paste required.

quickstart.comment: Log in by reading cookies from your active Chrome session
Sync stores, menu, slices, orders, rewards into the local SQLite store
See what slices are available right now across every Seattle store
Check your current reward balance and available rewards
Re-create your most recent order as a priced cart, without sending

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login --chrome reports 'no auth cookies found'
troubleshoots.fix: Open pagliacci.com in Chrome and log in. The CLI reads customerId and authToken cookies from the cookie store; if they're missing the session has expired.
Run `pagliacci-pp-cli auth status`. If cookies are stale, log in again at pagliacci.com and re-run `auth login --chrome`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty MenuSlices result during the day
troubleshoots.fix: Slices rotate daily and may be sold out before close. The endpoint reflects current availability at request time.
Stores have closed for the night. Use `stores list` for the next-day delivery scope or check `scheduling time-window-days <storeId> DEL` for upcoming windows.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user wants to interact with Pagliacci Pizza programmatically: browsing the menu, finding a store and time slot, building or pricing an order, checking rewards balance, or replaying a past order. The CLI is also the right choice for office-lunch automation (multi-address fan-out) and rewards-aware ordering (discount stacking).

recipes.title: What can I eat tonight?
recipes.explanation: Returns today's available slices across all stores with just the high-gravity fields for fast agent decision making.

recipes.title: Replay last order
recipes.explanation: Pulls your most recent OrderListItem, transforms it via OrderClone, re-prices it, and returns a sendable cart without submitting.

recipes.title: Maximize discount before checkout
recipes.explanation: Computes the best application of saved coupons, reward redemption, and account credit for a given order total.

recipes.title: Spend summary for last 90 days
recipes.explanation: Aggregates local order history into a single roll-up; --select narrows the response to high-gravity fields.

recipes.title: Delivery time for home address
recipes.explanation: One-call resolution from saved address label to next available delivery slot for the resolved store.
